title: Marks
intro: Drop a bookmark with `m{letter}` and jump back any time: `` `a `` (backtick) returns to the exact spot, `'a` (apostrophe) to the start of that line. Marks make round-trips across a file painless.

exercises.instruction: Drop mark `a` with `ma`, jump to the bottom with `G`, then snap back exactly with `` `a ``.
hints: `` ` `` (backtick) returns to the exact line AND column of the mark.

exercises.instruction: Mark this spot (`ma`), jump away with `G`, then return to the START of the marked line with `'a`.
hints: `'a` (apostrophe) lands on the line start; `` `a `` on the exact column.

exercises.instruction: From the bottom: mark with `ma`, jump to the top with `gg`, come back with `` `a ``.
hints: Marks survive any amount of moving around.

exercises.instruction: Delete back to a mark: `ma` here, jump to the line end with `$`, then `` d`a `` deletes everything in between.
hints: Deleting to a mark is exclusive — the character under the cursor survives.

exercises.instruction: Round trip with an edit: mark line 2 (`ma`), jump to the bottom with `G`, delete that line with `dd`, then return with `'a`.
hints: `'a` still works after edits elsewhere in the file.

exercises.instruction: Mark the accumulator line, inspect the top with `gg`, then jump back exactly with `` `a ``.
